metric_value: More than 300 telecom service providers aggregated

metric_value: Network aggregator providing unified managed remote access without owning telecom infrastructure

metric_value: Enterprise-oriented growth strategy targeting managed remote access outsourcing

metric_value: GoRemote acquired Axcelerant in late 2003 to add broadband fixed-location capabilities for teleworkers and branch offices
notes: Axcelerant had proven track record with Global 5000 enterprises. Combined companies cover fixed and mobile remote access.

metric_value: Universal access client providing simplified connection, login, VPN, firewall, and productivity apps regardless of connection type
notes: Windows-based. Works same on London dial-up as LA 802.11 wireless. Supports portal, single sign-on, auto-launch of applications.

metric_value: Integrated security agent providing policy management, enforcement, adaptive location-based protection, firewall, intrusion detection, automatic remediation
notes: VPN endpoints checked at each login. Automatic download of missing files, patches, and applications before allowing access.

metric_value: Fully managed VPN utilizing IPSec or SSL configured for remote workers, teleworkers, and branch offices
notes: Optional managed service attractive to SMEs seeking to offload firewall engineering, design, and 24x7 support.

metric_value: Universal Remote Control console providing real-time dashboard for network monitoring, security management, user provisioning, billing, and customer care
notes: Web-based IT management portal for outsourced remote user networks.

metric_value: Frame relay circuits at ~$600/month being displaced by DSL/cable broadband at $50/month with hundreds of dollars per site savings
notes: Major cost savings driver for fixed remote locations transitioning from legacy WAN services.

metric_value: Mobile workers rapidly displacing dial-up with broadband Ethernet at wireless access points and hotels
notes: No national single-source broadband provider exists. Workers paying a la carte at $10 per session.

metric_value: Aberdeen views GoRemote as offering the first unified solution for cost-effective management and control of fixed and mobile outworkers
notes: Outsourcing remote user data traffic outside the firewall becomes practical for the first time.

metric_value: Aberdeen projects multimillion-dollar savings from outsourcing remote access to GoRemote
notes: Enterprises advised to model the savings of outsourcing vs. DIY remote infrastructure.

metric_value: Cost savings on the order of 60% vs. do-it-yourself remote access infrastructure
notes: Managed service providers displace fixed asset investments in access servers, VPN hardware, firewalls, and authentication servers.

metric_value: GoRemote will guarantee service levels and commit to full deployment in less than 90 days

metric_value: Remote outworker usage is going to go up dramatically; the genie is out of the bottle

metric_value: GoRemote likely to garner increasing enterprise base with straightforward cost displacement proposition

metric_value: Unification and simplification of remote access services will drive telecom industry to offer better and more complete solutions

metric_value: Worker culture demands always online, always available connectivity for outworkers
notes: Frequent email, corporate application data, and Internet access required for both fixed and mobile categories.

metric_value: iPass acquired GoRemote Internet Communications Inc. for an undisclosed amount on Feb 16, 2006
notes: GoRemote was absorbed into iPass, which continued the managed remote access business.

metric_value: Pareteum Corporation acquired iPass Inc. in 2019
notes: iPass, which had acquired GoRemote in 2006, was itself acquired by Pareteum.

metric_value: Pareteum filed for bankruptcy in 2022 after SEC fraud charges; ultimate successor to GoRemote's business ceased operations
notes: The chain of acquisitions from GoRemote to iPass to Pareteum ended in corporate failure.

metric_value: COVID-19 pandemic forced massive shift to remote work, validating Aberdeen's 2003 prediction of dramatic outworker growth

metric_value: Outworker cost management identified as ripe topic for IT, procurement, and financial executives as part of overall telecom cost management strategy
